era_context: Late Bronze Age collapse aftermath; Egypt under Ramesses XI; Levantine early Iron Age; Phoenician expansion; Greece in early Iron Age. This is a period of significant political and cultural reorganisation across the Eastern Mediterranean.

events.title: Ramesses XI's Reign — Continued Decline of Central Authority
events.description: Ramesses XI continues to rule Egypt, with central authority continuing to decline. The High Priests of Amun at Thebes exercise increasing autonomy over Upper Egypt. No specific events are attested for -1013 in surviving sources.
events.certainty_note: Ramesses XI's reign is confirmed, but no specific events are attested for -1013. The year is assigned by chronological convention.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1013 is extremely sparse. No specific events are attested for this year. The chronology of this period remains uncertain by ±10–20 years.
